title: BugFinding
description: Standardized bug finding schema used across all pipeline stages. Fields are progressively enriched by each stage.

id.description: Unique identifier assigned by the Bug Hunter (e.g., bug-001)

found_by.description: List of agent identifiers that found this bug (e.g., claude-opus, codex-o3)

source_file.description: File path where the bug was found (source code audit)

line_range.description: Line range in the source file (e.g., 45-62)

url.description: URL where the bug was found (black box pentest)

request.description: Raw HTTP request that demonstrated the issue

response.description: Raw HTTP response demonstrating the issue

vuln_type.description: Human-readable vulnerability type (e.g., SQL Injection, XSS)

reasoning.description: Explanation of why this is a vulnerability and how it can be exploited

confidence.description: Confidence level that this is a real vulnerability

language.description: Programming language of the PoC (e.g., python, bash)

file.description: Path to the PoC file on disk

poc.description: Proof of concept (added by Strict Validator)

poc_file.description: Path to the expansion PoC file

demonstrated.description: Expansions proven via live PoC execution

reason_not_demonstrated.description: Why this expansion couldn't be demonstrated

theoretical.description: Expansions that couldn't be tested, clearly labeled

triager_notes.description: Triager's assessment notes (added by Strict Triager)

chains.description: Chain IDs this bug participates in (added by Bug Chainer)
